title: Iceberg bucket cluster_table_function_split_granularity out of bounds
description:
### Describe the bug
Easy to reproduce.
### How to reproduce
Create the table in Spark with [spark.py](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23777754/spark.py). Then, in ClickHouse run:
```sql
CREATE TABLE t0 ENGINE = IcebergLocal(local, path = '/var/lib/clickhouse/user_files/lakehouses/t0/test/t0', format = 'Parquet');
SELECT c0 FROM t0 SETTINGS cluster_table_function_split_granularity = 'bucket';
/*
May require running the SELECT query many times; it seems to be a race condition
assertion !empty() failed: deque::front called on an empty deque
*/
```
### Error message and/or stacktrace
Stack trace of a few threads:
```

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t1 (id INTEGER, name VARCHAR, value DOUBLE, created_at TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO t1 VALUES (1, 'test1', 10.5, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP), (2, 'test2', 20.0, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
CREATE TABLE t2 (id INTEGER, ref_id INTEGER, description VARCHAR);
SELECT t1.name, t2.description FROM t1 JOIN t2 ON t1.id = t2.ref_id;
key_bug_features: SELECT query with cluster_table_function_split_granularity set to 'bucket'
